name: ParkedFile
description:
Library component. Models the on-disk parked-<agent>.json file that gastown writes when a polecat parks (real source: `gt park` or equivalent). The file is the persistent record that survives both the Polecat process and the Deacon session: once written, it stays Persisted until explicitly cleaned up.

Lifecycle: NotPersisted (initial) -> Persisted (received Write). No transition back; the file isn't auto-removed in the bug-window timeframe. Real cleanup happens on `gt resume` after a successful unpark, but that's downstream of the bug we model.

This component is the load-bearing piece for the gastown #3587 fix: PrimeRecovery (the proposed `checkParkedWork` in `gt prime`) reads ParkedFile.Persisted as evidence that there's parked work to recover. Without ParkedFile, the fix would be observing in-memory state, which doesn't match the proposed real-world implementation. See gate-3587-defense.md P1.3.

WRITE_IN.description: receives a Write message from Polecat (or from `gt park`) when the polecat parks. Tagged 'Write'. Transitions NotPersisted -> Persisted.

actors.name: ParkedFile
actors.owner: Single-shot persistence record. NotPersisted -> Persisted on Write receive.

triples.event: Polecat parks; gt park writes parked-<agent>.json
terminal: file persists until cleanup (out of scope)
